item.yog-vlsi.vlsi_workbench.desc:
§7Design and fabricate redstone microchips.
§7Right-click to open the design interface.
§7Insert blank chips, Blueprints, or resources.
item.yog-vlsi.blueprint.desc:
§7Stores a chip design for sharing.
§7Use in a VLSI Workbench to import.
§7Craft an empty Blueprint, then Export from the workbench.
item.yog-vlsi.analog_cable.desc:
§7Carries a single analog redstone signal (0–15).
§7Connects Redstone Port to external redstone.
item.yog-vlsi.digital_cable.desc:
§7Carries up to 256 digital bits (64 channels × 4 colors).
§7Connects Digital Ports between ALUs.
item.yog-vlsi.redstone_port.desc:
§7Adapter between ALU and external redstone.
§7Place on any side of an ALU. Connect Analog Cable on one side, redstone on the other.
item.yog-vlsi.port_input.desc:
§7Place inside a chip's virtual circuit.
§7Right-click to cycle Input/Output/Bidirectional.
§7Sneak + right-click, then type a name in chat to rename.
item.yog-vlsi.port_output.desc:
§7Place inside a chip's virtual circuit.
§7Right-click to cycle Input/Output/Bidirectional.
§7Sneak + right-click, then type a name in chat to rename.
item.yog-vlsi.port_bidi.desc:
§7Place inside a chip's virtual circuit.
§7Right-click to cycle Input/Output/Bidirectional.
§7Sneak + right-click, then type a name in chat to rename.
item.yog-vlsi.alu_wood.desc:
§7Insert programmed microchips. Right-click to open the node editor.
§7Max chips: 2 | Digital channels/side: 8
item.yog-vlsi.alu_stone.desc:
§7Insert programmed microchips. Right-click to open the node editor.
§7Max chips: 3 | Digital channels/side: 16
item.yog-vlsi.alu_gold.desc:
§7Insert programmed microchips. Right-click to open the node editor.
§7Max chips: 4 | Digital channels/side: 24
item.yog-vlsi.alu_iron.desc:
§7Insert programmed microchips. Right-click to open the node editor.
§7Max chips: 5 | Digital channels/side: 32
item.yog-vlsi.alu_diamond.desc:
§7Insert programmed microchips. Right-click to open the node editor.
§7Max chips: 6 | Digital channels/side: 48
item.yog-vlsi.alu_netherite.desc:
§7Insert programmed microchips. Right-click to open the node editor.
§7Max chips: 8 | Digital channels/side: 64
item.yog-vlsi.chip_wood.desc:
§7Tier: §eWood§7 | §b5 ticks/s§7 | §a16×16 world
§7Place in a §6VLSI Workbench§7 to design circuits.
§7Ports: none (unprogrammed)
